title: Onoclea lineata
taxonomy: Onoclea lineata is classified within the family Blechnaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the chain ferns or water ferns. This family is part of the larger division Polypodiophyta, which includes all ferns. Within the Blechnaceae family, Onoclea lineata is placed in the genus Onoclea, which consists of several species of ferns. The genus Onoclea is further divided into two species, with Onoclea lineata being one of them. Onoclea lineata's classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Polypodiophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Blechnales, Family: Blechnaceae, Genus: Onoclea, Species: O. lineata.
uses: Onoclea lineata, also known as the lance-leaf lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves are rich in antioxidants, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help protect against cell damage. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. In addition, Onoclea lineata is believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

parts: Onoclea lineata is primarily used for its medicinal properties, with various parts of the plant being utilized. preparations: Onoclea lineata is also known as the lance-leaf fern or the smooth chain fern, and it has been used in traditional herbal remedies.
